REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, BOTH PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT SHALL BE DEPOSED AT LEAST 5 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE OF MAY 14, 2018. (IF TRIAL IS CONTINUED PARTIES MAY AGREE ON OTHER DEPOSITION DATES). PLAINTIFF SHALL BE DEPOSED FIRST; DEFENDANT SHALL BE DEPOSED THEREAFTER. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER ON MUTUALLY AGREEABLE DATES (IF PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON A DATE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF SHALL TAKE PLACE ON MAY 2, 2018 STARTING AT 9:00 A.M. AND DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT SHALL TAKE PLACE ON MAY 7, 2018 STARTING AT 9:00 A.M. PARTIES TO PREPARE ORDER AND PRESENT FOR EXECUTION (INCLUDING AGREED UPON DATES FOR DEPOSITION AND THE DATES PROVIDED BY THE COURT) AT THE TIME OF THE HEARING. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARIA MARANGHI'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY, DEEM REQUESTS ADMITTED, AND FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE COURT ADOPTS IN FULL THE RECOMMENDATION AND REASONS FOR THAT RECOMMENDATION IN THE THOUGHTFUL AND WELL-REASONED REPORT OF JUDGE PRO TEM MELINDA DERISH. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND ATTACHES THE REPORT OF JUDGE PRO TEM MELINDA DERISH AND SUBMIT IT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ANNE KIHAGI AND XELAN PROP 1, LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULTS AGAINST THEM IS GRANTED. MOVING DEFENDANTS HAVE SHOWN MISTAKE AND EXCUSABLE NEGLECT WARRANTING THE RELIEF THEY SEEK PER THE DISCRETIONARY RELIEF PROVISIONS OF CCP 473(B). MS. MARANGHI'S REQUEST FOR FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED. THOSE FEES AND COSTS WOULD HAVE BEEN AVOIDED HAD SHE AGREED TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT OF MS. KIHAGI AND NOT TAKEN THE DEFAULT OF XELAN WHEN SHE KNEW THAT THE MOVING DEFENDANTS WERE REPRESENTED AND INTENDED TO LITIGATE THIS CASE. MOVING PARTY TO SEND ORDER VERBATIM WITH THE TENTATIVE RULING BY WAY OF E-MAIL TODAY. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Karen Uchiyama, (415)563-9300 for Defendant Anne Kihagi.
Peggy Tsujimoto, (415)828-1919 CSR# 5229.

Argued by counsel for the Defendant. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:
Both Plaintiff and Defendant shall be deposed at least 5 days before the trial date of May 14, 2018. (If trial is continued parties may agree on other deposition dates). Plaintiff shall be deposed first; Defendant shall be deposed thereafter. Parties to meet and confer on mutually agreeable dates (if parties cannot agree on a date deposition of Plaintiff shall take place on May 2, 2018 starting at 9:00 a.m. and Deposition of Defendant shall take place on May 7, 2018 starting at 9:00 a.m. Parties to prepare order and present for execution (including agreed upon dates for deposition and the dates provided by the Court) at the time of the hearing. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, Deem Requests Admitted, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling, as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARIA MARANGHI'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, Deem Requests Admitted, And For Sanctions is denied in its entirety. The court adopts in full the recommendation and reasons for that recommendation in the thoughtful and well-reasoned report of Judge Pro Tem Melinda Derish.

Counsel for defendants is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and attaches the report of Judge Pro Tem Melinda Derish and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, Deem Requests Admitted, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Continued To Jan-10-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nancy M. Conway, Esq. of the Law Offices of Nancy M. Conway, T:415.241.1140, for plaintiff and moving party;
Karen Y. Uchiyama, Esq. T:415.563.9301, for defendant and opposing party.

Plaintiff's counsel did not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem; Defendant's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court vacated its tentative ruling and continued PLAINTIFF MARIA MARANGHI's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, Deem Requests Admitted, And For Sanctions to January 10, 2018 at 9:30 am in Department 302. The Judge Pro Tem shall prepare a Report and Recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, Deem Requests Admitted, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-30-2017 Continued To Dec-21-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Karen Uchiyama, Esq. 415-563-9301 for Defendant/opposing party.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is discussed. Defendant's Counsel appears late and requests a proposed order signed continuing the matter. The Court informs Counsel that a proposed order is not necessary on a matter that is continued and advised party to follow th ebriefing schedule. The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARIA MARANGHI's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, Deem Requests Admitted, And For Sanctions.

Plaintiff did not submit a separate statement with their motion. At the court's discretion this may be grounds for denial of a discovery motion. Mills v. U.S. Bank (2008) 166 Cal.App. 4th 871, 891-894. Plaintiff has provided no persuasive legal authority for disregarding CRC 3.1345. Plaintiff's motion is hereby continued to December 21, 2017 to give plaintiff the opportunity to comply with CRC 3.1345 and defendant the opportunity to respond. Plaintiff must file a separate statement by December 7, 2017. The court notes plaintiff's concern that defendant's opposition was filed three court days late and therefore offers plaintiff the option to file a supplemental reply by December 7, 2017. Defendant did not file Exhibit A to the Declaration of Karen Uchiyama, which was identified in defendant's opposition papers. Defendant is directed to file Exhibit A by December 1, 2017, and any response to plaintiff's separate statement by December 13, 2017. The parties must deliver courtesy copies of any new papers to Department 302 in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7 B.

Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/JPT/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2017 Set For Trial On Feb-26-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jun-22-2017 Off Calendar

Defendant Xelan Prop 1, Llc, Anne Kihagi'S Motion To Set Aside Default Under Code Of Civil Procedure 473(B) And (D)
Off Calendar May-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JULIE N. NONG, TEL. NO. 1-888-588-0428 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Anne Kihagi and Xelan Prop 1, LLC's motion to set aside the defaults against them is granted. Moving defendants have shown mistake and excusable neglect warranting the relief they seek per the discretionary relief provisions of CCP 473(b). While it appears that defendants' counsel made a series of mistakes that led to the entry of the default of Xelan and the failure to consummate an agreement by plaintiff Maria Maranghi to set aside the default of Ms. Kihagi, none of those mistakes, viewed singly or collectively, warrant depriving moving defendants of their ability to defend this case on the merits. Granting the motion is in accord with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. Ms. Maranghi has not shown that she will suffer any legal prejudice by the granting of the motion. Ms. Maranghi's request for fees and costs is denied. Those fees and costs would have been avoided had she agreed to set aside the default of Ms. Kihagi and not taken the default of Xelan when she knew that the moving defendants were represented and intended to litigate this case. Moving party to send order verbatim with the tentative ruling by way of e-mail today. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189
